purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
outputs: Bilingual human-guidance kernel and golden journeys.
Hierarchical help, static shell completion, safe CLI presets, and compact status with one priority next action.
Transactional local installer with check, plan, apply, custom home, rollback, and JSON modes.
Bilingual progressive-disclosure Observatory and updated documentation.
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
Targeted unit and E2E tests, npm run check, full npm test, doctor, package dry run, clean installer tests, golden English and Italian readability checks, and the strict story gate must all pass.

notes: Lead every user-facing response with outcome, impact, decision, protection boundary, and next action.
Keep stable codes and identifiers only in optional technical details.
Preserve Node 18.18+ and deterministic cross-platform behavior.

capability_bindings.name: repo
notes: Writes remain limited by the exact UX pull-request profile.

capability_bindings.name: node-test
notes: No network service or capability installation is required.

contextualization.summary: Make every user-facing Agentic SDLC journey understandable without prior plugin knowledge. Deliver a shared English/Italian human-first explanation kernel, hierarchical self-service CLI, safe presentation presets and shell completion, compact actionable status, transactional local installer and updater, and progressive-disclosure Change Observatory. Plain language must always precede optional technical details.

context_sources.excerpt: acceptance_criteria Effective configuration is versioned hash-locked explainable and migrated only through a reviewed plan hash; plugin upgrades cannot silently activate authority autonomy gate or budget defaults. A versioned workflow-definition and event-sourced workflow-instance engine supports software-project change-request technical-assessment and generic-governed-process presets without replacing the existing six SDLC execution phases. Requirement ceilings and a separate non-reusable delivery profile preserve an explicit autonomy choice for every pull request or local release with fail-closed action receipts and compatibility for existing v1 records. Deterministic enterprise fixtures exercise at least 1 000 stories 10 000 records 5 000 dependency edges and 100 000 trace events; orchestration and query remain within 2 seconds on Unix and 4 seconds on Windows warm Observatory reads remain within 100 milliseconds on Unix and 250 milliseconds on Windows and RSS remains within 256 MiB on Unix and 320 MiB on Windows. Trace events are durably hash-chained with integrity verification secret and PII protection is enforced at write and presentation boundaries and every CLI or Observato

# Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Build the first trustworthy enterprise foundation for the plugin project behavior must remain stable across plugin upgrades implementation sources must be discovered correctly large canonical project state must remain queryable within measured budgets and user-facing governance must be understandable without knowing the plugin's internal vocabulary. The canonical request is REQ-ENTERPRISE-CONTROL-PLANE-001-R2 (../../../requirements/REQ-ENTERPRISE-CONTROL-PLANE-001-R2.json) delivered here through story `ST-ENT-FOUNDATION`. ## Scope and non-goals Delivered in this story - fully materialized versioned project configuration with a hash-bound lock; - read-only migration preview followed by exact plan-hash application; - fail-closed behavior for drifted or invalid configuration while recovery reads remain available; - implementation-source discovery independent from knowledge-base search extensions; - a path-safe command-scoped canonical store; - a deterministic enterprise fixture and benchmark for 1 000 source files 1 000 stories 10 000 records 5 000 dependency edges and 100 000 trace events; - plain-language English and Italian guidance for

questions.question: What must appear before technical details?
questions.answer: Outcome, practical impact, any decision needed, what remains protected, and one next action in plain language.

questions.question: May internal terms appear in the primary explanation?
questions.answer: No. Terms such as bounded-autonomous, checkpointed, audit_only, profile, receipt, ceiling, hash, schema, codes, IDs, paths, and commands belong only in optional technical details.

questions.question: Can a CLI preset authorize or widen an operation?
questions.answer: No. Presets may change presentation-only allowlisted options and explicit CLI arguments always win; protected, mutating, authority, scope, path, command, and confirmation options fail closed.

questions.question: When may the installer write?
questions.answer: Only during apply after the exact current plan hash is supplied and revalidated under a lock. Check and plan are read-only, and invoking the installer without a mode is plan-only.

questions.question: What is the rollback boundary?
questions.answer: Plugin and marketplace bytes are restored together; optional global RTK configuration is explicit, separate, and outside that transaction.

questions.question: May this work add services, dependencies, production access, or secrets?
questions.answer: No. Use existing local runtime capabilities only; do not add services or dependencies and do not access production, secrets, or external systems.

constraints: Preserve Node.js 18.18+ compatibility, existing commands, existing --preset template behavior, and compatible JSON fields.

All new exports, generated completion scripts, plans, and machine-readable envelopes are deterministic and do not contain timestamps, process IDs, nonces, or temporary paths unless explicitly required as audit evidence outside the deterministic payload.
No user-facing primary section may require knowledge of Agentic SDLC internals.

approvals.summary: Approved exact final UX work brief against the immutable post-validation project snapshot, with delta evidence and one non-reusable PR delivery only.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Finalize only ST-ENT-UX against the new immutable current-state snapshot, one replacement contract, one non-reusable PR profile, and task start.
